previewBanner: Design preview — mirrors the production homepage. Route / is the shipped landing.

hero.title: What runs on your server?
hero.description: Appliance platform for your host. Storage, services, and administration run on the device — not in someone else's cloud.
hero.ctaDownloadHint: Install media ships when publicly released

heroStory.ariaLabel: Appliance signal path from hardware to control center

installJourney.heading: From install media to a running appliance
installJourney.intro: Planned first-boot flow — no invented media or timelines.

steps.title: Create boot media
steps.body: USB or equivalent from the published install image — only once it exists.

steps.title: Install on target hardware
steps.body: EFI-capable x86_64 host as the appliance target, not a container-only setup.

steps.title: Sign in on the device
steps.body: Login directly on the appliance, same-origin — no foreign admin cloud.

steps.title: Open control center
steps.body: Read-only overview with real API data; more modules only when the appliance supports them.

operateJourney.heading: Day-to-day after installation
operateJourney.intro: How docs and the control center work together — server operations, not SaaS ops.

steps.title: Set up storage
steps.body: Pools, shares, and persistent state under a clear boundary on the device.

steps.title: Manage services
steps.body: Start and monitor systemd-managed host processes on the hardware itself.

steps.title: Check status
steps.body: Overview and future modules only with real API data — never marketing metrics.

steps.title: Release changes
steps.body: Version changes only through published media and documented steps.

release.label: Release status
release.title: Public install media not available yet
release.body: Vyntrio OS is in active development. This preview shows the intended structure — no file, no version number, no invented timeline.

pillars.heading: Storage, services, control
pillars.intro: Three layers on one device: disk, processes, administration — no separate cloud layer.

storage.title: State on disk
storage.body: Persistent appliance state under a clear operations boundary — prepared for pools, shares, and controlled administration on the target device.

services.title: Processes on the host
services.body: Systemd-managed services on the hardware itself. No multi-tenant SaaS, no foreign control plane.

control.title: Local control center
control.body: After sign-in you get a read-only overview with real API data. Additional modules appear only when the appliance supports them.

surfaceShowcase.heading: The admin surface at a glance
surfaceShowcase.intro: This is what the control center looks like — static, no live data, same surface as after sign-in.

surfacePreview.heading: Vyntrio Control Center
surfacePreview.panelNote: Instance name, readiness, and modules appear only after successful sign-in to a real appliance.

productStatus.heading: What this page does not show
productStatus.body: Public pages describe the product. They do not fake operations, availability, or admin actions.
points: No live metrics on marketing pages
Live status only in the authenticated appliance view

finalCta.heading: Next steps
finalCta.body: When install media is ready, head to the release area. If you already run an appliance, sign in on the device.

hero.title: Install media and artifact status
hero.description: Release area for verified install images — no published file yet.

panel.heading: Artifact status
panel.intro: All entries are static. There is currently no published file to download.
panel.note: When media is available, verified artifacts with checksums and release notes will appear here — not before.

requirements.heading: What you need to install
requirements.intro: Planned minimum requirements for a target appliance device — without invented compatibility promises.

items.title: x86_64 hardware with EFI
items.description: Target platform is a dedicated host or server — not container-only deployment.

items.title: Bootable install medium
items.description: USB or comparable first-boot path — depends on the image published later.

items.title: Local network access
items.description: Initial setup and the control center run same-origin on the appliance, not through a foreign cloud.

inlineCta.heading: Back to product preview
inlineCta.body: Release details stay on this page. For appliance structure context, start on the preview homepage.

hero.title: Documentation for install and operations
hero.description: Planned topics for setup and day-to-day use — static in this preview, no live search.

sections.heading: First steps
sections.intro: Planned topics from install media to first sign-in on the device.

items.title: Install workflow
items.description: Steps from boot medium to a reachable appliance — ships with real media.

items.title: First sign-in
items.description: Local device login and transition into the control center with real API data.

items.title: Release notes
items.description: Version-specific changes only after an actual release — not before.

sections.heading: Appliance in daily use
sections.intro: Documentation for storage, services, and control — oriented like a server product, not a SaaS dashboard.

items.title: Storage & shares
items.description: Pools, shares, and persistent state under a clear operations boundary.

items.title: Host services
items.description: Manage systemd-driven processes directly on the hardware.

items.title: Control center
items.description: Read-only overview and future modules only when the appliance actually provides them.

inlineCta.heading: Continue to release preview
inlineCta.body: Documentation and install media stay separate. For artifact status, switch to the release area.

footer.tagline: Vyntrio OS — homelab appliance for storage, services, and control.
